text: The word translated ‘crucify’, here and in the following verses, is of uncertain meaning. If the Gabaonites kept the Mosaic law, they should have cut down a man ‘hanged on a tree’ the same day (Deut. 21.23); but it is not certain from the text that the bodies remained hanging. Some think the Hebrew verb signifies, not hanging, but throwing over a rock.

‘Elehanan son of Jaare’ is represented in the Latin by equivalent words, ‘Adeodatus (the God-given man) son of Saltus (the Forest)’. The word ‘embroiderer’ or ‘weaver’ has probably slipped into the Hebrew text by accident, since it occurs in the next line. In I Par. 20.5 it is the brother of Goliath, not Goliath himself, that is killed; as if the name were a kind of title, borne first by the giant slain in I Kg. 17 and then by his younger brother.
